parsed_review.summary: CP00-709 is a Risk C, 150-unit descriptor-only closeout pack extending RP23 External Integrations II (finance-integrations) over RP23.P05.M03.S19-RP23.P06.M00.S06, following the same validated pattern as CP00-694 through CP00-708. Independent read-only inspection confirms: (1) the 150-unit deliverable distribution (implementation:58, fixture:48, claude_review:7, security_audit:13, test:18, hermes_evidence:6) and micro-title distribution match the pack baseline and FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_CP709_REQUIREMENTS exactly, with correct first/last units and phase split (P05:144, P06:6); (2) the CP709 pack binding, manifest, contract latest_pack projection, and next-pack-queue all agree (next CP00-710 / RP23.P06.M00.S07, gates H23/C23), with CP694-708 preserved as historical; (3) no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and validateDescriptor (descriptor_only, no runtime execution, no financial payloads/credentials, no permission decision detail, no audit bodies, no real data, no runtime opening) and the contract validator writes only the spec contract file, not product state; (4) the authority boundary is intact (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-710, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false), asserted by model.test.js; (5) Hermes evidence packets are descriptor-only with emits_runtime_receipt=false, used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling. The pack honestly represents itself as production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false pending this review; nothing overclaims runtime or enterprise trust. Review-hardening checks pass: the pending_review state is expected because this is the gating review, auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON), prompt is 2680 bytes (<25.6KB), only Read/Grep/Glob are permitted, no forbidden tools, and no prior auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/tool-call-shaped review output exists. No P0, P1, or P2 findings. One P3 informational note on a benign changed-file-scope classification overlap. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approval authority; final human approval for enterprise trust is correctly out of scope for this receipt.

findings.title: new-session-handoff.md dual-listed as both untracked implementation and unrelated-preserved in changed-file-scope.json
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 181) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 188). This is a generic, repo-wide handoff document with a stale cursor snapshot (references CP00-082 / 2026-06-08) and makes no CP00-709 production_ready claim. It is correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs and active_pack_required_untracked_files, so it is not attributed as a CP709 deliverable. The dual membership reflects two independent classifications (file-type heuristic vs. pack-relatedness) rather than a scoping error, and is non-load-bearing for the pack's production_ready determination.
findings.recommendation: No action required for closeout. Optionally, clarify the scope-generator so a file flagged as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is not simultaneously emitted under untracked_implementation_files, to reduce reader ambiguity in future review packets.
